stack_overflow.title: Infinite recursion in render cycle causes Maximum call stack exceeded
stack_overflow.description: A component's render function calls itself without a termination condition, causing the call stack to overflow and crash the browser tab.

stack_overflow.title: RangeError: Maximum call stack size from recursive component update
stack_overflow.description: The component tree enters an infinite recursion during rendering. The call stack exceeds the browser limit and throws RangeError.

stack_overflow.title: Page crashes — infinite loop in component rendering
stack_overflow.description: The page freezes and then crashes. Console says Maximum call stack size exceeded.

stack_overflow.title: Browser tab crashes from recursive render
stack_overflow.description: Component keeps re-rendering itself infinitely until the browser gives up.

stack_overflow.title: page freezes and crashes, console says maximm call stack exeeded
stack_overflow.description: something is looping forever in the rendering. the page frezes and then crashes completly

stack_overflow.title: Application crashes with 'Maximum call stack' error
stack_overflow.description: Opening the page causes the browser to freeze and eventually crash. The error message mentions exceeding the maximum call stack size.

stack_overflow.title: Stack overflow: unbounded recursion in component render pipeline
stack_overflow.description: A render function recurses without a base case. The call stack grows until it exceeds the browser's limit, triggering RangeError.

stack_overflow.title: Component render loop cause stack overflow, page crash
stack_overflow.description: The component call itself in render again and again with no stop. Browser say Maximum call stack size exceeded.

array_bounds.title: Off-by-one error: accessing array element beyond length
array_bounds.description: The code accesses items[items.length] which is always undefined. Then calling toString() on undefined throws TypeError.

array_bounds.title: TypeError from array index out-of-bounds: items[length] is undefined
array_bounds.description: Classic off-by-one bug: code uses items[items.length] instead of items[items.length - 1] to get the last element. Returns undefined, crashes on method call.

array_bounds.title: List crashes on the last item — array index wrong
array_bounds.description: Something wrong with how the list accesses the last element. Crashes with undefined error.

array_bounds.title: Array off-by-one bug crashes item list
array_bounds.description: Accessing one past the end of the array. Undefined, then crash.

array_bounds.title: list crashes on last item, array index is off by one
array_bounds.description: the code is using items[items.length] insted of items[items.length - 1] and it crashes

array_bounds.title: Item list crashes when trying to format the last element
array_bounds.description: The list component crashes when it tries to display the last item. It seems like it's reading past the end of the array.

array_bounds.title: Array bounds error: items[items.length] returns undefined, not last element
array_bounds.description: The code to access the last item uses length as index instead of length-1. This returns undefined, and calling toString() on it throws TypeError.

array_bounds.title: Code use wrong index for last item in array, cause crash
array_bounds.description: The list crash because code use items[items.length] which give undefined. Should use items[items.length - 1].

array_bounds.title: Off-by-one: items[length] is undefined
array_bounds.description: Array index bug. Crashes on toString() of undefined.

cors_error.title: Cross-origin fetch blocked — server missing CORS headers
cors_error.description: A fetch request to an external API is blocked by the browser's CORS policy. The server doesn't include Access-Control-Allow-Origin in its response.

cors_error.title: CORS policy violation: cross-origin request rejected by browser
cors_error.description: The frontend makes a cross-origin fetch with custom headers, triggering a preflight check. The target server doesn't respond with appropriate CORS headers, and the browser blocks the request.

cors_error.title: External API call blocked by CORS
cors_error.description: Trying to fetch data from another domain but the browser blocks it. CORS error.

cors_error.title: CORS blocking the API request
cors_error.description: Browser won't let the fetch go through. Missing CORS headers on the server.

cors_error.title: cant fetch from external api, browser blocks it with cors eror
cors_error.description: the api call to the external service gets blocked by cors. the server doesnt send the right headers

cors_error.title: Integration broken: external API requests blocked by CORS policy
cors_error.description: Our integration with the external data provider is broken. The browser blocks all requests because the provider's server doesn't include CORS headers.

cors_error.title: CORS preflight failure: Access-Control-Allow-Origin missing from response
cors_error.description: Cross-origin fetch with custom X-Custom-Header triggers an OPTIONS preflight. The target server responds without CORS headers, so the browser rejects the actual request.

cors_error.title: Browser block request to other server, CORS error
cors_error.description: When application try to fetch from external API, browser block because CORS header missing. Server need add Access-Control-Allow-Origin.

network_timeout.title: API request aborted after timeout — AbortController cancellation
network_timeout.description: The fetch request to a remote endpoint was cancelled by AbortController after the timeout expired. The server didn't respond in time.

network_timeout.title: AbortError: fetch cancelled due to network timeout
network_timeout.description: The API call exceeded the configured timeout and was aborted via AbortController. The abort signal triggers an AbortError that propagates as a network failure.

network_timeout.title: API call timed out — request aborted
network_timeout.description: The request to the backend never came back. Got aborted after waiting too long.

network_timeout.title: Network request just hangs and then fails
network_timeout.description: The API call doesn't respond and eventually gets killed by the timeout.

network_timeout.title: Network failure: AbortController triggered after 2s timeout on unresponsive endpoint
network_timeout.description: The fetch call to the remote API exceeds the 2-second timeout. AbortController.abort() fires, producing an AbortError. User action cannot complete.

network_timeout.title: Request to API fail because timeout, abort signal fire
network_timeout.description: The API call take too long and get aborted. AbortController cancel the request after timeout. User cannot do anything.

network_timeout.title: API timeout — request aborted
network_timeout.description: Fetch cancelled by AbortController after timeout.

long_task.title: UI completely frozen for 3 seconds during heavy computation
long_task.description: A synchronous calculation blocks the main thread for 3 seconds. The entire page is unresponsive — no scrolling, clicking, or typing works.

long_task.title: Main thread blocked 3000ms by synchronous computation: Long Task violation
long_task.description: A CPU-intensive synchronous operation blocks the main thread for 3 seconds, far exceeding the 50ms Long Task threshold. All user interactions are queued.

long_task.title: Page freezes for 3 seconds — can't do anything

long_task.title: Long Task audit failure: 3000ms synchronous block on main thread

dom_explosion.title: Gallery inserts 5000 DOM nodes causing layout thrashing
dom_explosion.description: The gallery component renders all items at once without virtualization. 5000 DOM nodes are inserted synchronously, causing severe layout thrashing and jank.

dom_explosion.title: Synchronous insertion of 5000 nodes: forced reflow and render jank
dom_explosion.description: The gallery view appends 5000 elements to the DOM in a tight loop without requestAnimationFrame or virtualization. This triggers forced reflows and visible frame drops.

dom_explosion.title: Gallery loads everything at once — 5000 items, super laggy
dom_explosion.description: The gallery doesn't use any kind of lazy loading. It dumps all 5000 items into the page at once and everything gets janky.

dom_explosion.title: 5000 nodes inserted at once — page jank
dom_explosion.description: Gallery renders every single item with no virtualization. Major performance issue.

dom_explosion.title: galery is super laggy, loads all 5000 itms at once
dom_explosion.description: the gallery dumps all 5000 items into the page in one go. everything gets really janky and slow

dom_explosion.title: Gallery page causes browser to stutter with massive DOM
dom_explosion.description: Loading the gallery inserts thousands of elements all at once. The browser struggles to render them and the page becomes very laggy.

dom_explosion.title: Performance: gallery view inserts 5000 nodes without windowing
dom_explosion.description: The gallery component lacks virtualization. All 5000 items are appended synchronously in a loop, causing forced reflow warnings and noticeable frame drops.

dom_explosion.title: Gallery add 5000 element to page at same time, very slow
dom_explosion.description: Gallery put all 5000 item in page at once without virtual scroll. Page become very slow and laggy.

dom_explosion.title: 5000 DOM nodes, no virtualization — jank
dom_explosion.description: Gallery inserts all items synchronously. Forced reflow.

heavy_canvas.title: Image upload creates oversized canvas — 256MB memory allocation
heavy_canvas.description: The upload handler creates an 8000x8000 canvas for image processing without checking dimensions. This allocates 256MB of pixel data, risking tab crashes on low-memory devices.

heavy_canvas.title: Canvas memory spike: 8000x8000 getImageData allocates 256MB
heavy_canvas.description: The image processing pipeline creates a massive canvas and calls getImageData on the full 8000x8000 area. The resulting 256MB ImageData object causes significant memory pressure.

heavy_canvas.title: Image upload uses insane amount of memory
heavy_canvas.description: Uploading an image creates a huge canvas that eats up 256MB of RAM. Tab nearly crashed.

heavy_canvas.title: Upload handler eats 256MB for image processing
heavy_canvas.description: The image upload creates a massive canvas. Way too much memory used.

heavy_canvas.title: image upload uses way too much memory, 256mb for one image
heavy_canvas.description: the upload handler creates a giant canvas 8000x8000 and uses like 256mb of ram. almost crashed my browser

heavy_canvas.title: Browser slows down significantly after uploading an image
heavy_canvas.description: After uploading a photo, my browser became very sluggish. It seems like the image processing is using an enormous amount of memory.

heavy_canvas.title: Unbounded canvas allocation: no dimension check before 8000x8000 getImageData
heavy_canvas.description: The upload handler creates an 8000x8000 canvas and extracts full ImageData without checking source image dimensions first. 256MB allocation for any upload.

heavy_canvas.title: Upload create very big canvas, use too much memory for image
heavy_canvas.description: Image upload make canvas 8000 by 8000 pixel. This use 256 megabyte memory. Can crash browser on slow computer.

heavy_canvas.title: 8000x8000 canvas — 256MB allocation
heavy_canvas.description: Upload handler creates oversized canvas. No dimension check.

storage_quota.title: localStorage.setItem throws QuotaExceededError on large cache write
storage_quota.description: Attempting to store a large data payload in localStorage exceeds the browser's storage quota. The write fails with DOMException: QuotaExceededError.

storage_quota.title: DOMException QuotaExceededError: localStorage capacity exceeded by cache entry
storage_quota.description: The caching layer tries to write a 6MB entry to localStorage, exceeding the 5-10MB browser limit. The setItem call throws QuotaExceededError.

storage_quota.title: Can't save data — storage is full
storage_quota.description: Getting a QuotaExceededError when the app tries to save something to localStorage. Storage limit hit.

storage_quota.title: localStorage full — write fails with quota error
storage_quota.description: The app tries to cache too much data in localStorage and hits the limit.

storage_quota.title: localstorage is full, getting quota exceded eror when saving
storage_quota.description: the app tries to save somthing to localstorage but it fails because theres no space left

storage_quota.title: App fails to save preferences — shows no error to user
storage_quota.description: My settings aren't being saved. It turns out localStorage is full but the app doesn't tell me anything — it just silently fails.

storage_quota.title: Storage capacity: localStorage.setItem fails on 6MB payload (5MB browser limit)
storage_quota.description: The cache layer attempts to write a ~6MB string to localStorage. This exceeds the typical 5MB browser quota, throwing QuotaExceededError. No fallback to IndexedDB.

storage_quota.title: LocalStorage full, cannot save data, no error message to user
storage_quota.description: Application try save big data to localStorage but fail because storage full. No message show to user about problem.

stale_closure.title: Event handler shows stale state value — closure captures old reference
stale_closure.description: A click handler displays 0 instead of the current counter value 5. The handler closed over the initial state and doesn't see subsequent updates.

stale_closure.title: Stale closure bug: handler captures initial state, misses 5 updates
stale_closure.description: The event handler captures the counter variable by value at creation time. After 5 state updates, the handler still references the original value 0.

stale_closure.title: Counter shows 0 when it should be 5
stale_closure.description: Clicked the handler after updating the counter 5 times but it still shows 0. Classic stale state bug.

stale_closure.title: Handler shows wrong value — stale state
stale_closure.description: The click handler shows 0 instead of the actual count of 5.

stale_closure.title: handler shows 0 insted of 5, its using the old value
stale_closure.description: the event handler is stuck on the old state value. counter is 5 but it shows 0

stale_closure.title: Display shows incorrect count after multiple updates
stale_closure.description: I updated the counter 5 times but when I check the value it still shows 0. The display doesn't reflect the actual current state.

stale_closure.title: React closure gotcha: handler captures stale reference to counter state
stale_closure.description: The event handler closes over the initial counter value (0). Five rapid state updates occur, but the handler's closure still references the stale value.

stale_closure.title: Handler show old value 0, real value is 5, not update
stale_closure.description: Event handler show counter as 0 but actual value is 5. The handler not see new value because it remember old one.

stale_closure.title: Stale closure: shows 0, actual is 5
stale_closure.description: Handler captured initial state. Doesn't see updates.

zindex_conflict.title: Dropdown menu renders behind sticky navigation bar
zindex_conflict.description: The account dropdown has z-index:50 but the sticky nav has z-index:100. The dropdown opens but is hidden behind the navigation.

zindex_conflict.title: Z-index stacking context: dropdown (50) occluded by nav (100)
zindex_conflict.description: The account menu dropdown's z-index is lower than the sticky navigation header's. The dropdown renders in the correct position but is visually hidden behind the nav.

zindex_conflict.title: Can't see the dropdown — it's behind the nav bar
zindex_conflict.description: The account dropdown opens but it's hidden behind the top navigation. Can't click any of the menu items.

zindex_conflict.title: Dropdown hidden behind header
zindex_conflict.description: The menu dropdown is behind the sticky nav. Z-index issue.

zindex_conflict.title: dropdown menu is behind the nav bar, cant see or click it
zindex_conflict.description: the acount dropdown opens behind the navigation bar. cant acess settings or logout

zindex_conflict.title: Account menu is inaccessible — hidden under navigation
zindex_conflict.description: When I click my account avatar, the dropdown menu appears to open but I can't see it or interact with it. It's behind the top navigation bar.

zindex_conflict.title: CSS z-index conflict: dropdown at z-50 rendered below fixed nav at z-100
zindex_conflict.description: The account dropdown's z-index (50) is lower than the sticky navigation's (100). The dropdown is positioned correctly but the stacking context places it behind the nav.

zindex_conflict.title: Dropdown menu go behind navigation, cannot click menu item
zindex_conflict.description: Account dropdown open but hide behind top navigation bar. Z-index of dropdown lower than navigation. Cannot access settings.

zindex_conflict.title: Z-index: dropdown behind nav
zindex_conflict.description: Dropdown z-50, nav z-100. Menu inaccessible.

layout_shift.title: Late-loading banner causes 120px content layout shift
layout_shift.description: A promotional banner injects itself below the navigation 1.5 seconds after page load. All content shifts down by approximately 120 pixels.

layout_shift.title: CLS violation: promotional banner injected without space reservation
layout_shift.description: A banner element is dynamically inserted below the nav after a 1.5s delay. No space is reserved in the layout, causing a ~120px shift that affects all below-fold content.

layout_shift.title: Page jumps down when the promo banner loads
layout_shift.description: Everything shifts down suddenly when a banner appears at the top. Really jarring while scrolling.

layout_shift.title: Content shift when banner loads late
layout_shift.description: A promo banner pops in after the page loads and pushes everything down.

layout_shift.title: page jumps when a baner loads late, pushes evrything down
layout_shift.description: the page content suddenly moves down when a promotional banner apears. very annoying when your reading

layout_shift.title: Frustrating page jump — reading content shifts mid-scroll
layout_shift.description: I was reading content on the page when suddenly everything jumped down because a banner loaded late at the top. Lost my place completely.

layout_shift.title: Layout instability: dynamic banner insertion causes ~120px CLS event
layout_shift.description: A promotional banner is injected into the DOM 1.5s post-load without a placeholder. This creates a layout shift of approximately 120px, exceeding CLS thresholds.

layout_shift.title: Banner load late and push all page content down
layout_shift.description: Promotional banner appear after page already loaded. Push everything down about 120 pixel. Very bad for reading.

layout_shift.title: 120px layout shift from late banner
layout_shift.description: Promo banner injects without reserved space.

unhandled_promise.title: Background sync promise rejects without catch handler
unhandled_promise.description: A background synchronization operation rejects with 'connection reset' but has no catch handler. The error is silently swallowed unless monitored globally.

unhandled_promise.title: Unhandled rejection: Promise.reject without .catch in sync module
unhandled_promise.description: The background sync module fires a promise that rejects with a connection reset error. No .catch() or try/catch is present, triggering an unhandledrejection event.

unhandled_promise.title: Background sync fails silently — no error shown
unhandled_promise.description: Something in the background sync broke but there's no error message. Data might be stale.

unhandled_promise.title: Promise rejection not caught — sync error silent
unhandled_promise.description: Background sync promise rejects but nothing catches it. Silent failure.

unhandled_promise.title: backgrond sync fails with no eror message, data might be stale
unhandled_promise.description: the background sync thing broke but theres no error shown anywhere. had to check the console to find it

unhandled_promise.title: Data appears stale — possible background sync failure
unhandled_promise.description: I noticed my data wasn't updating. It turns out the background sync is failing but the app doesn't show any error or notification about it.

unhandled_promise.title: Silent failure: background sync Promise.reject with no error boundary
unhandled_promise.description: The sync module's promise chain has no rejection handler. When the connection resets, the error goes to the global unhandledrejection event but isn't surfaced to the user.

unhandled_promise.title: Background sync fail but no error show, user not know
unhandled_promise.description: Synchronization in background fail with connection error. No catch handler exist. User not see any error message.

unhandled_promise.title: Unhandled promise rejection — sync fails silently
unhandled_promise.description: Background sync rejects without catch. No user notification.

listener_leak.title: 100 duplicate scroll listeners accumulate without cleanup
listener_leak.description: Each component mount adds a new scroll event listener but never removes the previous one. After multiple navigations, 100+ duplicate listeners cause scroll jank.

listener_leak.title: Event listener memory leak: scroll handlers not removed on unmount
listener_leak.description: The component registers a scroll listener on mount but doesn't clean up on unmount. After 100 mount/unmount cycles, 100 duplicate listeners degrade scroll performance.

listener_leak.title: Scroll gets laggy after navigating around — listener leak
listener_leak.description: The more you navigate between pages, the laggier scrolling gets. Listeners piling up.

listener_leak.title: 100 scroll listeners piled up — performance tanking
listener_leak.description: Scroll handlers aren't being removed. 100+ duplicates after navigating around.

listener_leak.title: scroll gets lagier the more you navigate, listners not cleand up
listener_leak.description: every time you go to a page a new scroll listener gets added but never removed. after a while theres like 100 of them

listener_leak.title: Page performance degrades over time during normal use
listener_leak.description: The longer I use the app, the more sluggish scrolling becomes. It seems like something is accumulating that slows things down.

listener_leak.title: Memory/performance leak: scroll addEventListener without removeEventListener
listener_leak.description: Each component mount calls addEventListener('scroll') without a corresponding removeEventListener on unmount. After 100 navigations, 100+ handlers fire on every scroll event.

listener_leak.title: Every time go to page, new scroll listener add but not remove
listener_leak.description: Component add scroll listener when mount but not remove when unmount. After navigate many time, too many listener make scroll slow.
